commit.message:
Fix bot 409 conflict: retry with backoff instead of crashing

When Telegram returns 409 (another getUpdates request), wait
5-30s and retry instead of crashing the process, which caused
Docker to instantly restart and repeat the conflict loop.

→ Remotion (compositor) → Next.js studio UI.

commit.message:
security: Payload-Limits, Optimistic Locking, Template-Validierung

Fix 1 - Payload-Size-Limits (DoS-Schutz):
- nodes: max 500, edges: max 2000, triggers: max 50
- Gilt fuer PUT /api/flows/[id] und POST /api/flows
- Klare Fehlermeldung bei Ueberschreitung (400 Bad Request)

Fix 2 - Optimistic Locking (Concurrent-Edit-Schutz):
- Server gibt nach jedem erfolgreichen PUT das neue updated_at zurueck
- Client speichert updated_at in serverUpdatedAtRef
- Bei manuellem Speichern wird expected_updated_at mitgeschickt
- Server prueft: stimmt DB-Zeitstempel mit expected_updated_at ueberein?
  Falls nicht => 409 CONFLICT mit klarer Fehlermeldung
- Autosave sendet kein expected_updated_at (last-write-wins fuer gleichen Tab)
- 409 wird permanent angezeigt bis der Nutzer die Seite neu laedt

Fix 3 - Template-Validierung (Daten-Integritaet):
- Zod-Schemas fuer templateNodeSchema, templateEdgeSchema, templateTriggerSchema
- Code-Templates und DB-Templates werden vor Nutzung validiert
- Bei Validierungsfehler: Fallback auf defaultPreset + console.error
- Verhindert dass korrupte Templates alle neuen Flows zerstoeren

commit.message:
test: add regression tests for mission completion summary writer

Covers mission_write_completion_summary() with 41 assertions:
- Error handling (missing file, missing DB)
- Metrics accuracy (completed, failed, fixed, blocked, superseded)
- Self-correction rate edge cases (0%, 50%, 100%)
- Average duration calculation (excludes failed tasks)
- Success criteria parsing (checked, unchecked, uppercase [X])
- Slug sanitization in output filename
- Mission title extraction with fallback
- Atomic write (no temp files left behind)
- Overwrite behavior on re-run

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix nav pill clipping: use w-max to prevent fixed-position width constraint

The fixed-position nav with left:50% constrained the pill's width,
causing flex items to overflow the content area regardless of padding.
Adding w-max on the pill forces it to size to its full content + padding,
preventing the Book Now button from being clipped by overflow-hidden.

feat(feat-021): LLM 调用参数格式排查修复

修复内容：
1. LLMService 类构造函数新增 temperature 和 max_tokens 参数
2. chat() 方法优先使用实例默认参数（支持 kwargs 覆盖）
3. get_llm_service() 函数新增 temperature 和 max_tokens 参数
4. 所有 API 端点（ppt.py, generate.py, history.py, config.py）接收并传递 LLM 参数

参数映射：
- 前端传递：temperature (0-2), max_output_tokens
- 后端存储：temperature (0-100 整数), max_output_tokens
- LLM 调用：temperature (0-2), max_tokens

统一处理逻辑：
- 前端传递的参数优先
- 未传递时使用默认值（temperature=0.7, max_tokens=4000）
- OpenAI 服务商额外支持 response_format 参数

commit.message:
fix: Multiply maxWidth by screen scale factor for Retina-sharp rendering

maxWidth is specified in logical points by consumers, but the resize needs
to produce enough pixels for @2x/@3x displays. Uses a fixed 3.0 scale
(highest common iOS factor) to guarantee sharp images on all devices.

Overhaul rider model: sideways stance, segmented limbs, realistic animations

- Longer arms that can reach the board for grabs

commit.message:
feat: add task-type affinity scoring to worker task selection

Workers now prefer tasks matching their historical strengths. Before
claiming, _compute_task_affinity() queries per-tag success rates from
the tasks table and scores pending tasks accordingly. Workers with
high success rates on e.g. [INFRA] tasks will preferentially claim
[INFRA] work. Falls back to standard FIFO when no history exists or
only one task is pending.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: bind-mount host NVIDIA Vulkan libs into ECS containers for Isaac Sim

The ECS GPU AMI's nvidia-container-toolkit (v1.0.0) doesn't auto-mount
Vulkan/graphics libs into containers. The host UserData already installs
libnvidia-vulkan-producer.so, but containers couldn't access it.

Fix: bind-mount host /usr/lib64 and /usr/share/vulkan/icd.d into
containers as /host-nvidia-libs and /host-vulkan-icd. The VULKAN_SETUP_CMD
copies the host's nvidia_icd.json and ldconfig registers host libs.

Changes:
- run.sh: Add nvidia-host-libs/nvidia-host-vulkan volumes + mountPoints
  to all 4 task definitions (training, replay, sim2sim, shell).
  Add LD_LIBRARY_PATH=/host-nvidia-libs env var. Simplify VULKAN_SETUP_CMD.
- replay.sh: Use host-mounted Vulkan libs instead of apt-get libnvidia-gl.
- Dockerfiles: Remove libnvidia-gl-550-server, fix ICD to point to
  libnvidia-vulkan-producer.so instead of libGLX_nvidia.so.0.
- setup.sh: Sync UserData with launch template v3 (Vulkan producer install).

commit.message:
Add cross-file intra-package call resolution and query disambiguation

Go parser now stores unresolved direct function calls as a property on
calling nodes. A new linker phase (linkCalls) resolves these against
same-package functions in different files, creating EdgeCalls edges with
kind=cross_file. This captures call relationships like ParseFile ->
ExtractDocument that were previously missed because buildCallMaps only
indexed functions within the same file.

Also adds --package flag to `query edges` and prints a disambiguation
warning when multiple nodes match a name, guiding users to narrow results.

commit.message:
fix: move @types/* and typescript to dependencies for Render production build

Render sets NODE_ENV=production before the build command runs, causing npm
to skip devDependencies. Moving all build-time packages (@types/*, typescript,
ts-node) to dependencies ensures they are always installed.

Also reverts render.yaml buildCommand back to plain `npm install && npm run build`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix push notification pipeline: response checking, diagnostics, auto-subscribe banner

Root cause: usePushSubscription.subscribe() never checked the server response.
If /api/push/subscribe returned 401/500 (e.g., table missing, auth failure),
the client still set subscribed=true — user thought push was enabled but
DB had no subscription data, so no notifications were ever sent.

Fixes:
- usePushSubscription: check res.ok, rollback browser subscription on server failure
- usePushSubscription: add step-by-step console.log debug logging
- subscribe API: log actual upsert error details (message, hint, code)
- test endpoint: full step-by-step diagnostic (session → VAPID → table → subs → send)
- sw.js: add push event debug logging
- PushPromptBanner: auto-prompt on login, dismiss for 24h, mount in layout
